title: rhinotropis cornuta var. fishiae
intro: Rhinotropis cornuta var. fishiae, also known as Fish's Buckwheat or Desert Buckwheat, is a type of flowering plant in the buckwheat family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rhinotropis cornuta var. fishiae include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing antiseptic and antibacterial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, and capsules, with some traditional uses including treating fever and sore throats.
